Job Title: Head of Individual Life Business Development & Specialty Markets
Job Description: Head of Individual Life Business Development & Specialty Markets Role purpose: This role is an Executive leadership position accountable for setting and significantly influencing the strategic direction of Guardian’s Individual Life Insurance business growth. Reporting to the Head of the Life Insurance Business, the Head of Life Business Development & Specialty Markets is responsible for Strategic Growth of the Life Insurance business including expanding into new markets, and ensuring the Life business remains the earnings and revenue powerhouse of Guardian . The role operates with high autonomy , leads through senior leaders, and makes decisions in ambiguous, complex, and highly regulated environments with material impact on Guardian’s long‑term performance and value. You will: Set and Drive Growth Strategy Set and execute Guardian’s Life Insurance business development and specialty markets strategy , shaping where and how the Life business grows Drive growth into new strategic markets that strengthen Guardian’s reach, impact and long‑term economic power Make strategic tradeoffs balancing growth, profitability, risk, capital deployment, and brand impact across the Life business Own Go‑to‑Market Strategy & Enterprise Alignment Lead the new marketplace go‑to‑market strategy with strong alignment across all functions within the Life Insurance business Resolve cross‑functional conflicts and competing priorities to ensure execution at scale Serve as a senior integrator across functions, ensuring strategies translate into measurable commercial outcomes Maintain Product Positioning, Brand, and Franchise Value Ensure Life products maintain strong competitive positioning and client‑centricity , supported by Guardian’s brand and reputation Influence the evolution of the Life portfolio, leading with Whole Life Insurance and a competitive dividend , while supporting innovation and disciplined portfolio evolution Partner with Product Management, Underwriting, and Actuarial leaders to ensure long‑term sustainability and value creation Expand Strategic Markets & Accelerate Growth Beyond the Core Lead expansion into strategic and specialty markets to supplement core growth and drive Life sales Grow Bank‑Owned Life Insurance (BOLI) and the flexible premium (UL/VUL) product lines Expand into new strategic markets where Guardian can win through differentiation Enable Transformation & Future‑Ready Growth Leverage transformational enablement (technology, data, distribution capabilities, and operating model evolution) to build forward‑looking Life solutions Champion paradigm‑shifting Life solutions for emerging generations , including Gen Z, ensuring Guardian’s relevance over the long term Anticipate external market, demographic, and regulatory shifts and proactively reposition Guardian’s Life growth strategy accordingly Lead Teams & Drive a High-Performance Culture Operate with enterprise mindset , shaping decisions beyond immediate functional boundaries Own growth outcomes, investment prioritization, and resource allocation tied to Life Business Development and Specialty Markets Leading through subordinate leaders, developing executive‑level talent and strengthening organizational capability Serve as a trusted advisor to senior executives on Life growth strategy and market expansion decisions You are: An experienced commercial executive with deep Life Insurance expertise, recognized for generating new and innovative solutions to complex problems , leading in ambiguity, and delivering sustained growth at scale. You are known for: Setting strategic direction rather than executing predefined plans Making high‑impact decisions with incomplete information Influencing outcomes through leadership, not authority alone Building durable capabilities that outlast individual initiatives. Having an external mindset, with a deep awareness of market dynamics and evolving trends in distribution, product, solutions, and the end client You have: 10+ years of experience in Life Insurance, business development, distribution strategy, or specialty markets, including 5+ years in executive or senior enterprise leadership roles Deep expertise in Life Insurance products, underwriting and pricing dynamics, dividends, and regulatory frameworks.  Demonstrated success driving growth, market expansion, and distribution productivity Experience leading through senior leaders in highly matrixed organizations Strong strategic and financial judgment grounded in long‑term enterprise performance Bachelor’s degree required; MBA or advanced degree preferred Salary Range: $155,370.00 - $255,255.00 The salary range reflected above is a good faith estimate of base pay for the primary location of the position. The salary for this position ultimately will be determined based on the education, experience, knowledge, and abilities of the successful candidate. In addition to salary, this role may also be eligible for annual, sales, or other incentive compensation. Job Description: Overview Every day, Guardian helps our 29 million customers realize their dreams through a range of insurance and financial products and services. Our Purpose, to inspire well-being, guides our dedication to the colleagues, consumers, and communities we serve. We know that people count, and we go above and beyond to prepare them for the life they want to live, focusing on their overall well-being — mind, body, and wallet. As one of the largest mutual insurance companies, we put our customers first. Behind every bright future is a Guardian. Learn more about Guardian at guardianlife.com. Guardian has set out an ambitious growth strategy over the next 5 years, referred to as our Journey To ’30.  This strategy focuses on continuing the growth of businesses by diversifying our product portfolio, increasing our distribution footprint, growing our customer base, and leveraging technology, data and AI as a strategic enabler. This growth strategy requires ongoing transformation across the enterprise, including both the business lines and corporate functions. Guardian is seeking a highly analytical, execution-oriented individual to serve as Corporate Development Leader . In this high-impact role, you will lead Guardian’s inorganic growth initiatives through mergers & acquisitions and strategic investments, shaping the future of our enterprise. As Corporate Development Leader , you will operate within Guardian’s Enterprise Strategy & Corporate Development team , responsible for shaping enterprise strategy, driving inorganic growth, and leading M&A, venture investments, and strategic partnerships. You will play a critical role in high-visibility initiatives across the following areas: M&A and Strategic Investments Source and evaluate acquisitions/divestitures, investments and JV opportunities aligned with Guardian’s strategic priorities Lead end-to-end transaction processes, including financial modeling, valuation, due diligence, and deal structuring Develop investment cases and secure approvals through Guardian’s governance process Negotiate term sheets and definitive agreements in collaboration with legal and finance teams Market Intelligence Analyze industry trends and competitive dynamics to inform inorganic growth strategy Build and maintain relationships with investment bankers, advisors, and industry executives to generate deal flow Prepare executive-level presentations and recommendations for senior leadership and the Board Integration Support Partner with business leaders and integration teams to ensure successful post-close execution and synergy realization Team Leadership – Mentor junior team members and help develop the team as a talent pipeline for Guardian You have: Bachelor’s degree (Finance, Economics, Business or related field); MBA or CFA preferred 10+ years of experience in investment banking, private equity, and/or corporate development Sector expertise within Financial Services or Insurance required Meaningful M&A transaction experience Very strong quantitative and analytical skills (including proven ability to build models from scratch and modify models crafted by others) High level of proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel, Word, and PowerPoint Ability to work well independently under pressure and to tight deadlines, and have the aptitude to synthesize large amounts of information and to develop innovative solutions Strong communication skills effectively (both oral and written), especially with senior and board level leadership Strong interpersonal and influencing skills, organizational savvy and judgment; ability to navigate at all levels of the organization and achieve goals with matrix teams and resources Salary Range: $178,690.00 - $293,560.00 The salary range reflected above is a good faith estimate of base pay for the primary location of the position. The salary for this position ultimately will be determined based on the education, experience, knowledge, and abilities of the successful candidate. Job Title: Client Management Market Leader, West Region - Advisory
Job Description: Position Summary Do you have a track record of success leading a seasoned high performing team? Do you think big and grow, accelerate impact, and lead change?  Is a culture where “People Count,” “We Do the Right Thing,” and “We Hold Ourselves to Very High Standards” important to you?  If so, Guardian is seeking a Client Management Market Leader for our large market and advisory business segment. The Client Management Market Leader – Advisory West (CMML) provides critical leadership and direction to a team of seasoned Client Executives (CE) in the pursuit of overall client satisfaction, growth, retention, and profitability objectives for groups with 2000+ lives. The CMML is responsible for ensuring a high-performance mindset with his/her assigned team and executing strategies that focus on client management discipline, consistency, and best practices. In addition, the CMML will manage and maintain book-of-business assignments to maximize team productivity and effectiveness. The CMML will set the goals for each member of his/her assigned team consistent with Guardian’s overall goals, provide performance management and coaching, and work closely with each individual to create annual development plans. You are: A leader who sets high personal performance standards and leads by example. You have a history of building strong team alignment within a large market strategy and building key stakeholder partnerships both internally and externally. A collaborator who can deeply engage with other CMML peers and can leverage Home Office relationships across the organization to help meet their assigned goals and objectives. You have: Education: Four-year college degree or equivalent work experience Experience: 5 years of group insurance experience preferred 3 years leadership in Account/Service Management Demonstrated understanding of group and large market business and strategy In-depth knowledge of and working experience supporting brokers and employers/plan holders. Must achieve required industry licensing within 90 days of date of hire and maintain license through completing Continuing Education requirements. You will: Have an Achievement Orientation: The capacity to be energized and excited by challenging goals. It is reflected in a true sense of competitiveness and drive for results. Ability to adapt to change and lead through change: Capacity to relate change to market/customer/employee needs to increase understanding and acceptance Projects optimism around change Involves others in change Demonstrates emotional intelligence Builds trust and loyalty Inspires action and commitment Create followership to drive positive change Resilient Demonstrate Purposeful Action: Acting proactively, which includes going beyond what is required for the job. It includes a willingness to act decisively and boldly to drive the business forward and lead the way to improved processes and services. Reporting Relationships: As our Client Management Market Leader – Advisory West, you will report to our Head of Client Management – Advisory & Key Markets, who reports to our Group Benefits Head of Client Management. Location and Work Arrangement: Successful hire must be located within the Central, Mountain, or Pacific time zones. Preferred locations include the following Guardian Offices: Plano, TX; Newport Beach, CA; Kansas City, MO; or Chicago, IL. Work arrangement will be hybrid (partial in-office presence; partial work from home). Travel: Approximately 20% travel required for this position Salary Range: $107,440 - $147,730 The salary range reflected above is a good faith estimate of base pay for the primary location of the position. Job Title: Group Benefits Sales Representative - Seattle, WA
Job Description: Guardian is seeking a Group Benefits Sales Representative who will be responsible for building and maintaining broker and plan holder relationships to drive ongoing sales and retention of groups of 2-1,999 lives with a primary focus between 100-1,999 market. Success will be determined based on the ability to attain high levels of production through broker development and internal relationships! This role will report to the Market Leader within the assigned territory. You will: Market, position, and sell Guardian’s entire Group Benefits portfolio products and technology solutions to your designated brokers. This includes Dental, Vision, Life, Disability, Absence, and Supplemental Health products. Support the general wellbeing of the broker and client relationship for the development and growth of Guardian sales and client retention. Understand territory dynamics to build, develop and maintain broker relationships to grow the book of business while representing Guardian’s vision to inspire well-being. Be accountable for consistently achieving or exceeding personal sales production expectations by having a clear understanding of products and creating and implementing tactical sales strategies. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to examine market trends and stay ahead of consumer demands. Represent Guardian by acting as a market leader, sharing thought leadership, and maintaining strong community relationships. Identify, recommend, and champion process improvements and organizational initiatives to increase sales of product offerings. Achieve required industry licensing within 90 days of date of hire and maintain license through completing Continuing Education requirements. You Have: A BS, BA degree or equivalent industry experience. Minimum of 4 years group insurance sales experience or a combination of Guardian work experience, education and/or related extracurricular activities. Experience and knowledge of relevant industry products and processes including, but not limited to Worksite, LTD, Dental products, complex absence products, online enrollment, experience rating, dental network analysis processes. Soft Skills: Collaborative team player with the ability to effectively build rapport in person and virtually. Exercise sound judgment and integrity in a changing, matrixed, and fast past environment. Exude confidence, positivity, and proactivity. Act with accountability to deliver on commitments with speed and quality. Location and Travel Requirements: Up to 60% travel within the territory Successful hire will be based in the assigned territory (Seattle, WA area) ​ Salary Range : $40,000 The salary range reflected above is a good faith estimate of base pay for the primary location of the position. Job Description: The HR Associate supports the HR Business Partner (HRBP) function by delivering high quality, operational HR support across the employee lifecycle. This role serves as a key connector between employees, people leaders, and HR Centers of Expertise (COEs), ensuring consistent execution of HR processes, accurate‑ data management, and responsive employee support within a regulated insurance environment. You will Key Responsibilities Coordinate and execute core HR lifecycle activities including onboarding, offboarding, transfers, promotions, and organizational changes. Support HRBPs during key talent processes such as performance management cycles, annual compensation reviews, succession planning, and workforce planning activities. Prepare HR materials, documentation, and presentations for HRBP led‑ discussions with business leaders. Track and manage action items, timelines, and deliverables related to HRBP initiatives and client needs. Serve as a point of contact for routine employee and manager inquiries, escalating more complex issues to the HRBP as appropriate. Partner with HR Centers of Excellence and Shared Services teams to coordinate services and resolve issues impacting HRBP client groups. Identify opportunities to streamline HRBP workflows and improve operational efficiency. Participate in special projects and initiatives as assigned. You have Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field, or equivalent experience. 2-4 years of experience in an HR, HR operations, or administrative support role. Working knowledge of HR fundamentals across the employee life cycle Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines. Strong attention to detail, organizational skills, and follow through. Ability to handle sensitive information with discretion and professionalism. Strong written and verbal communication skills.  Demonstrates the capability to communicate effectively across all levels of the organization. Strong  analytical and project management skills. Proficiency in MS Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint). Proven ability to be flexible and adaptable in the face of changing circumstances. (Agility) Experience and an invested interest in leveraging data to influence decision making outcomes. (Data Judgment) Preferred Qualifications Prior experience supporting HR Business Partners or HR leaders. Familiarity with HR systems (HRIS) and standard HR processes. Experience supporting performance, compensation, or talent management cycles. Key Skills & Competencies Operational excellence and follow through Professional judgment and discretion Strong coordination and execution skills Collaborative, service-oriented‑ mindset Comfort working in a fast paced, deadline-driven environment Location Hybrid: 3 days in a Guardian office location. 2 days WFH Salary Range: $59,110.00 - $88,660.00 The salary range reflected above is a good faith estimate of base pay for the primary location of the position. Job Title: Dental Network Data Analyst- Recruitment Support Specialist
Job Description: As the Dental Network Data Analyst- Recruitment Support Specialist , you will play a critical role in enhancing the performance and efficiency of the recruitment team by providing data-driven insights , ensuring high-quality lead management, and maintaining the integrity of recruitment-related data systems . This position helps to accelerate the recruitment process by equipping recruiters with accurate, actionable information to make timely and informed decisions. You Are Able to communicate in a professional and sales-oriented manner Well versed and comfortable with using data to tell a story Excellent with written and oral communication skills Naturally analytical A strong critical thinker with excellent listening skills Excellent with attention to detail Able to work in a fast-paced, data-focused environment with cross-functional teams Someone with a great work ethic and capacity for issue resolution You Will Analyze and scrub lead data to ensure accuracy and completeness before routing to recruiters. Extract and synthesize data from multiple sources (e.g., field staff reports, termination reports, Netminder, P&R systems, and termination data) to support recruitment strategies. Perform research on providers in areas from different sources and present clean presentable information to recruitment staff Format and load lead data into the Source of Record (SOR) and configure appropriate task sets for recruiter workflows. Call providers to get any missing documentation to ensure a complete contract Communicate with providers regarding their contract and status Confirm newly contracted providers signed the state appropriate agreement Work closely with Provider Network Recruitment to communicate and follow-up on missing contract elements Track the life cycle of the contract ensuring completion once the provider has completed application, following up on any delays with the processing team Deliver accurate, complete contract packets to the Provider Relations Administration for final processing. Input contract data into provider databases while ensuring the accuracy of the data Submit complete and accurate contracts to Provider Relations Administration for processing Work closely with the recruitment team for any field preparation including conferences and events by preparing mailings, shipping logistics, contract support, as well as data support while they are in the field, not limited to verifying provider status. Perform other duties and projects as required You Have Minimum associate’s degree, bachelors preferred or equivalent work experience 2-3 years related experience, preferably in the insurance industry, a professional health office, and/or in an analytics driven administrative assistant role. Strong data and analytical background Proficient in Excel, CRM platforms, and data visualization/reporting tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, etc.). Strong Microsoft Office skills Location/Travel This role is remote, preferably either PST or CST zone hours, with occasional quarterly travel to the nearest Guardian Life hub/Office. Job Description: We are seeking a strategic, collaborative, and entrepreneurial leader for Guardian’s Enterprise Strategy team. In the highly visible role of Enterprise Strategy Leader, you will help develop the long-range plans for Guardian and partner with senior leaders to identify, develop, evaluate, prioritize and deliver strategic initiatives across the organization.  You will lead internal consulting engagements to solve critical strategic questions for the Guardian Leadership Team, working with senior sponsors, business units, and corporate functions, while partnering closely with Enterprise Strategy associates and analysts. A successful candidate will work across the Enterprise Strategy & Corporate Development team to provide strategic guidance and deliver on high-visibility strategy projects.  You will utilize strategic frameworks to structure problem-solving, direct quantitative and qualitative analyses of operational and financial data, and oversee industry/ competitor research to continuously evaluate Guardian’s positioning. The role reports to Guardian’s Head of Strategic Growth & Planning.  You will work with the Corporate Development Team, as well as the leaders of business units and corporate functions. You will also coach and develop our Enterprise Strategy analysts and associates where appropriate. Your work will span across: Enterprise-wide strategy development – Work to define the strategic direction, with both near-term and long-term priorities for Guardian.  This will be supported by research, analytics, and logical problem solving and strategic frameworks. Internal strategy consulting – working with a senior sponsor, execute deep-dive strategy projects on high priority topics for the business units and corporate functions Growth initiatives and innovation – assess or develop business cases for new ideas driving innovation, partnerships, and other areas of investment outside of BAU. Identify, frame, and implement specific cross-cutting strategic initiatives that are critical to the success of Guardian’s overall strategy. Development and delivery of strategic content for senior leadership – In partnership with Chief Strategy Officer, develop agenda, content, and analysis for key strategic fora, including the annual Strategy Session of the Board and the annual Guardian Leadership Team Strategic Planning offsite Strategic engagement – connect with business unit leaders and functional leaders to conduct two-way dialogue regarding Guardian strategy – sharing identified plans and opportunities and listening for feedback and challenges.  You will use both formal and informal channels to engage with leaders and Guardian colleagues. You are: A strategic problem-solver, with demonstrated experience in using frameworks and analysis to make data-driven decisions or recommendations for questions that don’t have easy answers. Experienced in executing strategy projects – including scoping, managing timelines, and conducting/ overseeing analyses in order to deliver actionable, impactful recommendations. An effective influencer with strong interpersonal skills, maturity, and judgment to influence senior stakeholders and cross-functional teams A confident communicator (both oral and written) with experience in presenting to senior audiences in highly visible roles Highly organized and efficient, with ability to prioritize across multiple projects and competing demands at any given time; focus on efficiency with your own time, team meetings, and senior stakeholder discussions Results-oriented and pragmatic; looking to make real, tangible impact and ensure that recommendations can be effectively operationalized Intellectually curious and innovation minded; always asking “what’s next?” You have: Bachelor’s degree from a top institution with strong academic performance and demonstrated leadership ability; MBA from top-tier business school preferred, but not required 10+ years of experience in roles related to management and strategy consulting, business management or internal corporate strategy functions Advanced proficiency with Microsoft PowerPoint and Excel Experience in insurance and/ or financial services is preferred, but not required Experience at a top management consulting firm is preferred, but not required You will: Shape the direction of a highly innovative, growing company with many strategic opportunities Be exposed to and influence the agendas of Guardian senior executives Develop expertise in a variety of functions and businesses across the company Be mentored and coached by experienced leaders on problem solving, communications, and leadership skills Salary Range: $178,690.00 - $293,560.00 The salary range reflected above is a good faith estimate of base pay for the primary location of the position. The Advisory Market Account Implementation Manager , primarily handles complex and large group implementations, providing overall coordination and support to employers and brokers during the onboarding and set-up of customer’s new benefit plans/policies. This includes owning the implementation timeline, gathering required information, ensuring accurate data entry into systems, as well as coordinating with other departments to ensure a smooth transition for the new customer/policy. You will: Attend Finalist meetings when required and facilitate regularly scheduled meetings with the customer, broker, and internal teams throughout the implementation process. Serve as the single point of contact for internal and external partners throughout the implementation process, answer inquiries, resolve issues timely and ensure effective transition to service teams at the end of the implementation. Evaluate the needs of new customers and provide consultative solutions during implementation to ensure details are understood and documented and that the plan administration setup fully meets customer expectations. Develop, communicate, maintain, and manage the detailed implementation project plan, ensuring deadlines and milestone dates are met and hold partners accountable to meet agreed upon dates. Maintain knowledge and awareness of all Guardian group products (Life, Disability, Absence (including State Paid Leave programs), Supplemental Health, Dental, Vision etc.), processes and system capabilities to effectively educate customers and communicate service capabilities. Facilitate the timely gathering of implementation information and enrollment data to ensure the ability to pay claims by the agreed upon effective date and accuracy of the first bill. Effectively collaborate and coordinate with internal and external teams (e.g., Data Exchange team, Ben Admin partners, Enrollment Technology Consultants, Sales and Client Management teams, Case Set-Up team, billing, and enrollment teams etc.) to ensure a successful implementation and that administrative systems and vendor platforms are set up timely and correctly. Use standard methods for own and teamwork, finding ways to enhance processes and improve customer experience. Manage complex implementations and monitor customer satisfaction throughout the implementation process. Mentor and assist with training of team members as needed. Participate in department and strategic initiatives as needed. You have: 3-+ years of experience in the group insurance industry; implementation or client management experience preferred. Experience managing complex implementations for employer groups with 1000+ employees. Knowledge and experience with FMLA and PEO implementations. Knowledge of Benefit Administration platforms and data exchange formats. Project management skills. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. The ability to work independently and make decisions. The aptitude to promote consumer centric decision making and the ability to lead and adopt change. Bachelor’s Degree preferred or equivalent work experience in business, operations, or related field. Location: This is a mobile (remote) position. Job Description: The Business Transformation Enablement Analyst supports strategic initiatives across the Wealth Management organization by helping translate business needs, field insights, and enterprise priorities into clear, structured, and actionable deliverables. This role partners closely with Business Transformation leadership and delivery teams to support analysis, intake, documentation, and prioritization activities that enable informed decision‑making and effective execution. You will Analysis & Synthesis Gather, organize, and analyze inputs from multiple sources, including business stakeholders, field feedback, leadership direction, and delivery teams. Support the synthesis of complex and sometimes ambiguous information into clear summaries, themes, and insights. Assist in translating business questions and requests into structured problem statements and analytical artifacts to support leadership discussions and decisions. Business Transformation Enablement Support Business Transformation by maintaining accurate, well‑organized documentation for both in‑flight and new initiatives. Help ensure initiatives are clearly defined and positioned for effective handoff to delivery teams. Contribute to the development of transformation summaries, roadmaps, and planning materials. Support the intake of new ideas, requests, and initiatives entering the Business Transformation team. Help document initiative objectives, scope, assumptions, dependencies, and expected outcomes. Maintain intake documentation, trackers, and artifacts that support prioritization and transparency across initiatives. Stakeholder Engagement & Communication Assist with synthesizing stakeholder and field feedback into clear takeaways and emerging themes. Draft and update communications, summaries, and presentation materials for business and leadership audiences. Support the preparation of executive‑ready materials, including presentations, dashboards, and briefing documents. Project & Delivery Support Support initiative planning and execution by maintaining timelines, tracking milestones and dependencies, and managing action items. Assist with risk and issue tracking and status reporting across assigned initiatives. Partner with cross‑functional teams to ensure information is accurate, current, and accessible. Support governance routines by maintaining logs, documentation, and follow‑up items. You have BA/BS degree required. 3–5 years of experience in business analysis, transformation support, project coordination, business operations, or related roles. Prior experience or foundational knowledge in Wealth Management and/or Insurance, including familiarity with advisors, financial products, and business operations. Working knowledge of project management concepts such as scope tracking, dependency management, risk and issue tracking, and status reporting. Strong analytical and organizational skills with the ability to synthesize information from multiple sources. Clear and concise written and verbal communication skills. Comfort working with ambiguity, evolving priorities, and multiple concurrent initiatives. Ability to collaborate effectively with senior leaders, peers, and cross‑functional teams. Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint, Teams). Experience supporting intake, prioritization, or governance processes is a plus. Job Description: Job Description: Job Summary Senior Data Engineer (T4) will support the design, development, and maintenance of enterprise grade data solutions for Guardian’s Investment Data platform - Investment Data Mart (IDM). This role is responsible for building scalable data pipelines, ensuring high quality data delivery, and partnering with business and technology teams to support Investments domain needs across asset classes such as Fixed Income, Equity, Commercial Mortgage Loans, Derivatives Products etc. The candidate must have deep technical expertise in modern data engineering, excellent problem solving skills, and the ability to work in an Agile environment. K ey Responsibilities Requirements Elicitation & Analysis Understand product vision, business requirements, data models, and platform objectives. Collaborate with Product Owners, SMEs, and Architects to define technical requirements and data mapping specifications. Translate business rules into detailed low level technical designs (LLD). Project Planning, Tracking & Reporting Estimate efforts for design, build, and test activities for assigned modules. Provide input to sprint planning, schedule creation, and dependency identification. Track and report progress, highlighting risks, blockers, and scope variations. Ensure timely, high quality delivery of assigned work items. Design Develop low level designs for ingestion, transformation, and delivery pipelines. Evaluate alternate solutions and recommend optimal technical approaches. Present LLDs in formal reviews and incorporate feedback. Development & Support Build scalable, high performing ETL/ELT pipelines using approved tools and coding standards. Implement complex investment data transformations involving trades, positions, valuations, pricing, and security master data. Adhere to version control, code management, and DevOps practices. Perform peer reviews; at T4 level, act as a primary reviewer for junior engineers. Testing, Debugging & Validation Participate in test case walkthroughs and validate coverage for functional and non functional requirements. Conduct impact analysis for enhancements and defects. Lead debugging efforts for complex data or performance issues; escalate risks as needed. Documentation Create and maintain technical documentation, including data mappings, transformation logic, unit test results, and operational runbooks. Ensure documentation meets SDLC and audit requirements. Process, Compliance & Governance Follow established SDLC, data governance, security, and audit frameworks. Support internal and external audit requests related to the data platform. Ensure engineering practices adhere to corporate standards. Technical Leadership (T4 Expectation) Serve as a subject matter expert for Investments data flows, platform components, and system integrations. Coach and mentor team members through training sessions and knowledge sharing forums. Contribute to reusable frameworks, knowledge repositories, and best practices. Qualifications & Required Skills Must Have Technical /Process related Skills Strong experience in SQL , performance tuning, and large scale data processing. Experience building ETL/ELT pipelines with robust orchestration and error handling logic using ETL tools like Informatica (IICS or IDMC) Hands on experience building data platforms for R eporting. Strong Python skills and good hands-on experience on Databricks platform and PySpark . Understanding of investment data domains such as Fixed Income, Private Equity, S ecurities, T rades, P ositions, D erivatives, P ricing, Derivatives etc. Knowledge of relational and dimensional data modeling. Experience with CI/CD pipelines , version control tools , scheduling tools ( Git, Control-M etc.). Experience of working in Agile teams (Scrum/Kanban) preferred Nice to have: Machine learning experience Education & Experience Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science , Engineering, or a related field. Typically 5-8 years of experience in data engineering roles for T4 levels. Behavioral Competencies Analytical and detail oriented with strong problem solving skills. Highly collaborative and comfortable working with cross functional teams. Effective in communicating technical concepts to business stakeholders. Self driven with an ownership mindset and focus on delivery excellence. Adaptable and able to thrive in a fast paced, Agile environment. Additional Information This role is part of Guardian’s Investments Technology organization . Occasional evening or weekend work may be required during critical releases or production support windows. This position oversees the effective delivery of both virtual and in-person engagements, intern and rotational assignments, as well as learning and development activities. The ideal candidate is a strong project manager who demonstrates exceptional communication and teamwork skills and is dedicated to supporting talent and operational excellence within a dynamic IT setting. Key Responsibilities: Operational Support Support daily workforce planning and development initiatives. Manage elements of workforce strategy, such as career pathing documents and resource repositories like SharePoint. Monitor and report KPIs for onboarding, rotations, and learning programs. Plan and execute both internal and external events, including conferences, hackathons, workshops, skilling sprints, and summer internship programs. Coordinate all aspects of virtual and on-site event logistics such as venue arrangements, preparation of materials, and communication with attendees. Manage logistics for rotation programs, including schedule coordination, communications, placement administration, and performance monitoring. Partner with HR and business units to advance early talent recruitment and professional development initiatives. Communication Planning and Execution Help create and manage the team's communications calendar and content. Find and develop opportunities for recognition, celebrating achievements, or sharing best practices. Promote engagement throughout Digital and Technology by supporting a culture of continuous learning and the implementation of early talent and learning programs. Post announcements, highlight learning opportunities, and amplify key messages across social communication tools and internal platforms ( e.g. Viva Engage, Sharepoint ). Three to five years of professional experience in project coordination, communications, or workforce development roles. Demonstrated proficiency in managing logistics for events, upskilling initiatives, or talent development programs. Knowledge of IT organizational frameworks and workforce requirements is considered advantageous . Skills: Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) and collaboration tools (Teams, SharePoint). Experience using generative AI ( e.g. Microsoft Copilot) in communications and project management a plus Strong project management capabilities. Ability to generate reports and dashboards using Excel or visualization tools (e.g., Power BI) preferred. Strong organizational and time management skills with attention to detail. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. Ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders effectively. Collaborative mindset and proactive problem-solving approach. Adaptability and willingness to learn in a dynamic, fast-paced environment. Job Description: As the Vocational Rehabilitation Consultant will develop cost effective strategies to assist individuals with disabilities in returning to the workplace. The VRC will work with Guardian plan holders, employees, and physicians to coordinate return to work opportunities through job accommodations and return to work strategies. The Vocational Rehabilitation Consultant provides assessment of LTD, STD and Life claims, to determine claimants’ level of functionality and provide evaluation of work capabilities or independence in activities of daily living, according to contractual definitions. The Vocational Rehabilitation Consultant identifies claimants that may be eligible for return-to-work planning and assistance, transitional work, job modification, and/or vocational rehabilitation programs. The VRC must possess a thorough working knowledge of medical diagnoses and treatment modalities, criteria supporting vocational feasibility, and a clear understanding of the interface of medical pathology and employment functionality. You are A highly motivated and reliable individual who can work with varying levels of supervision – independently or collaboratively. You are detail-oriented and a decisive decision maker who can manage multiple priorities at the same time with a positive attitude. You can collaborate with stakeholders to provide effective case management within the boundaries of contractual definitions. You will Assess claimants medical, psychological condition, social and economic situation, aptitudes, work experience, restrictions and limitations, educational background, and behavioral/motivational factors to determine viability of and probability of success of vocational rehabilitation opportunities.  Compare this assessment with policy rehabilitation provisions and make cost/benefit analysis to develop programs to assist individuals with disabilities in obtaining employment. Maintain proactive dialogue with claimants, plan holders and treatment providers to formulate viable return to gainful work plans.   Cost effective utilization of vendors to provide rehabilitative services to support program goal.  Coordination with public services/governmental agencies to control costs and identify similar benefits. Collaborate with Disability Clinical & Vocational Services staff and case managers to move toward an appropriate plan to reduce the duration of disability and enhance probability of RTW. Serve as educational resource on vocational rehab aspect of claims. Educate parties involved in claim management regarding the extent of functional limitations and impact on ability to perform work activities. Utilize standard vocational resources effectively to perform occupational assessments; perform transferable skills analyses and labor market surveys to assist Claims Department in benefit eligibility determinations.  Compare medically supported restrictions and limitations with job/occupational requirements to determine claimant’s residual functionality. Apply knowledge of the ADAAA to identify effective work-related accommodations and stay-at-work options to our plan holders and their employees. Work with plan holders to identify/effectuate appropriate worksite modifications and transitional work plans to help return claimant to employment. Case management of part-time work claims to increase full-time return to work potential, as appropriate. Utilize vocational expertise within a flexible environment to support Guardian’s current products (STD, LTD, Life, and ADA), as well as future products, to meet business needs. You have 5 or more years of actively providing vocational rehabilitation/case management services to disabled individuals in an insurance setting Insurance industry experience strongly preferred Master’s Degree required, preferred in Rehab Counseling Case Management experience strongly preferred Receipt of, or eligibility for, certification in profession, such as Certified Rehabilitation Counselor (CRC), Certified Disability Management Specialist (CDMS), Certified Case Manager (CCM). Excellent verbal and communications skills Ability to exercise independent & sound judgment in decision making Strong problem-solving, analytical, clinical, and information Excellent time management & organizational skills Demonstrated ability to multi-task with the ability to manage continually changing priorities and the ability to prioritize work based on customer service needs and departmental regulations Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and OASYS, ERI, OOH, BLS Location This is a mobile position where preference may be given to candidates within commuting proximity to a Guardian office with the ability to travel to Guardian offices as determined by the People Leader. Travel Occasional 0- 10% of the time. Salary Range: $68,970.00 - $113,310.00 The salary range reflected above is a good faith estimate of base pay for the primary location of the position. Job Description: The Workforce Management Real-Time Analyst plays a critical role in ensuring operational stability and meeting service-level agreements (SLAs) across multiple queues and work types. This role is responsible for monitoring intraday performance, adherence, and staffing levels in real time — while making proactive decisions and communicating with operations partners to protect service delivery. With strong analytical skills, clear communication, and a pulse on day-to-day operations, the Real-Time Analyst is the front line of SLA management and business continuity. You are Calm, focused, and decisive in fast‑moving environments, with the ability to assess real‑time conditions and act quickly to protect service levels. Highly analytical, using data and trends to anticipate risk, identify performance drivers, and support sound decision‑making. Clear and confident communicator who can translate real‑time performance insights into actionable guidance for operations partners and leaders. Proactive and solution‑oriented, taking ownership to address intraday challenges before they impact customers or SLAs. Collaborative and trusted, building strong partnerships with operations, scheduling, and leadership teams to maintain business continuity. Detail‑oriented with a strong sense of urgency, balancing multiple queues, priorities, and work types without losing sight of the bigger picture. Customer‑focused, understanding how real‑time decisions directly impact customer experience and operational outcomes. You will Monitor live volumes, staffing levels, adherence, and SLA performance across all key channels and queues (phone, back office, digital, etc.). Take proactive action when SLAs are at risk — including re-skilling, priority adjustments, escalation protocols, and stakeholder engagement. Provide timely and clear updates to operations leaders when performance risks arise. Recommend or initiate short-term moves to stabilize service. Queue Management & Work Prioritization: Adjust workflow allocations to ensure the highest-priority items are addressed within target timeframes. Manage work overflow scenarios. Adherence & Shrinkage Management: Monitor schedule adherence and unplanned shrinkage. Partner with team leads to ensure coverage is aligned to forecasted need. Reporting & Insights: Generate and share daily performance reports, trend summaries, and insights. Highlight key drivers of performance or risk. Schedule Support: Assist with scheduling of on and off phone events, PTO, and identifying coverage gap identification to maintain intraday balance. Tool & Data Proficiency: Leverage WFM platforms (e.g., Verint, NICE, Genesys, or similar), Excel dashboards, and reporting tools to drive real-time decisions. You have 2+ years of experience in workforce management, contact center operations, or real-time queue monitoring. Foundational understanding and proficiency with Excel (formulas, conditional formatting, pivot tables, etc). Excellent verbal and written communication skills. Detail-oriented with a high level of ownership and urgency. Experience in a contact center environment. Understanding of Service Level and ASA, levers that impact it, and how to prioritize accordingly. Reporting and data insights, and the ability to communicate those insights Ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment. Familiarity with WFM tools (Genesys, Verint preferred) and ticketing systems. Location This role is a remote role. Job Title: Change Management and Quality Assurance Lead
Job Description: The Change Management and Quality Assurance Lead role is accountable for leading quality assurance and regulatory execution across provider credentialing, recredentialing, and provider data management activities. This role ensures Guardian meets delegated credentialing audit requirements, maintains regulatory compliance, and delivers consistent, high‑quality outcomes through disciplined quality programs and effective change management. You will lead a team of Quality Assurance Specialists while partnering closely with credentialing, network services, compliance, and technology stakeholders. Through strong project management, documentation rigor, and oversight of regulatory‑driven process changes, you will help ensure Guardian’s operations remain compliant, scalable, and aligned with evolving regulatory expectations, including increased use of automation and AI‑enabled capabilities. You Will: Lead and develop a team of Quality Assurance Specialists responsible for credentialing and provider data quality oversight. Establish and maintain quality assurance programs supporting credentialing, recredentialing, and provider demographic and directory data changes. Ensure timely and successful completion of delegated credentialing audits, partnering with internal stakeholders to address findings and sustain compliance. Project manage regulatory‑related process changes, ensuring requirements are clearly translated into operational execution. Serve as the primary point of contact for regulatory‑driven changes impacting credentialing and network services processes. Document and maintain Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for new or updated processes, implementing follow‑up controls to measure adherence and effectiveness. Ensure clear, timely communication of procedural changes to impacted teams, supporting understanding and consistent adoption. Partner with technology and operations teams to support quality assurance for new tools, automation, and AI‑enabled solutions as human tasks are replaced or augmented. Monitor regulatory trends and provider‑facing notice requirements, ensuring appropriate operational, technology, and communication updates are coordinated and executed. Support continuous improvement by identifying quality risks, control gaps, and opportunities to strengthen regulatory and operational outcomes. You Have: Experience leading quality assurance or regulatory operations in a healthcare, insurance, or provider network environment. Strong knowledge of credentialing, recredentialing, and provider data management processes. Proven ability to project manage regulatory or compliance‑driven process changes from requirement through implementation. Job Description: The Enterprise Data Solutions & Operations (EDSO) team within the Digital & Technology (D-T) function is looking to staff a Head of Data Solutions (the ‘Role’) for our FPRS/CSWM line of business. This role will work closely with core FPRS/CSWM business value streams (e.g., distribution mgmt., shop, purchasing, service, claims, etc.) to define, shape, prioritize and lead the delivery and evolution of key data and information product (‘Products’) solutions supporting business objectives. More specifically, this role will drive the capture and planning for new demand of features and/or enhancements to Guardian’s data Products as articulated by key business leaders from one or more key value stream and/or the respective FPRS/CSWM FP&A functional BI leader. These data Products include everything from consumer-facing analytics (reports/dashboards), to inter/intra-application data services (APIs/extracts/shares), to data services supporting AI/GenAI automation, to the underlying data definitions and reference data that define the FPRS/CSWM businesses. In addition, this role will have accountability for planning and delivering strategic data initiatives within one or more FPRS/CSWM value stream, collaborating with the value stream Digital & Tech (D&T) Leader, the value stream Business Leader, the value stream Product Delivery Managers (PDMs), and the value stream Product Owners(POs)) to identify/scope/size and execute key data needs, and to identify and raise new opportunities for data activation/adoption. A key aspect of the role will be to balance the needs across value stream demands, soliciting/recommending priorities, and in turn, incorporating and aligning these priorities into a roadmap and plan for delivery, including resource planning (business SMEs, UAT participants, etc.), training, and activation in support of full value realization. Finally, the FPRS/CSWM Data Solution Head will direct the data steward(s) in their charter to continuously capture metadata related to all data elements (e.g., definition, quality standards, calculation logic, SLA requirements, etc.), and to report/coordinate any data quality issues/SLA breaches/definitional changes to the same. From a strategic management perspective, the role will bring a keen understanding of value stream processes, supporting applications and key data elements. Moreover, the role will establish and nurture relationships with both value stream executive leadership, as well as functional and analytic SMEs in the value stream teams to keep a pulse on the latent needs and direction of the business. Using these relationships, the role will anticipate future data needs, constantly updating and communicating the backlog of features to be addressed in the data/info product roadmap - all in close coordination with business partners in the FPRS/CSWM FP&A analytics team(s). From an org management perspective, the Role will directly manage and grow a team of data product owners (DPOs).  These DPOs will be aligned with FPRS/CSWM value stream data products and will drive the execution of new build features/enhancements to their respective data products. In addition, the Role will manage both direct (EDSO FPRS/CSWM Solution Engineering team members, EDSO FPRS/CSWM Data Product Owners) and indirect (D&T Solution & Data Architecture resources, and D&T Data Governance resources), in order to drive the successful delivery of data products, as well as to help foster the development of the next generation of D&T data leaders, You are: You are a highly independent senior manager with the ability to (or rapidly become) conversant in life insurance and wealth management products and their respective business processes. You have an aptitude for using and shaping data solutions in support of business objectives and are able to clearly articulate the value that can be attributable to data/info products. You are highly organized, staying abreast of multiple stakeholders’ priorities, the status of multiple parallel initiatives/projects/data products, and the state of value realization and adoption for production data assets. You communicate fluidly and extemporaneously, able to articulate how data solution functionality will meet business needs in a brief, non-technical manner. While you may not be a recent ‘hands on keyboards’ technologist (you may even have come from a business or analytical background), you have a deep understanding of data and analytic technologies, architectural principles, and you aren’t afraid to dig into data models and pipeline logic. Finally, you are reliable in terms of your commitments, honest and brave with issues/escalations, and have a client service mentality. You have: 15+ years of experience with an escalating competency in data and analytic solutions BS or BA required; MS or MBA preferred 3+ years working in an agile environment 3+ years of experience managing staff/teams, potentially (preferably) with geographically diverse teams, often organized on a product delivery basis (highly matrixed) 5+ years of experience with standard program management tools (such as JIRA, AgileCraft, ATIIM, etc.), as well as desktop collaboration/ideation tools (Sharepoint, MSOffice, OneDrive, Lucid, Draw.io, etc.) Experience in life insurance and wealth management products and industry preferred (e.g., whole/term/universal/supplemental/etc., as well as , FIA, portfolio/client management tools for FAs/Brokers, etc.) Experience with enterprise data management principles such as data ownership/stewardship (experience as a data domain steward or data product owner would be a plus) Ability to document and capture business requirements in detail, including metric calculation logic, quality/timeliness expectations, analytic functionality, hierarchies, etc., as well as functional user journeys as they relate to data solutions A client focus with strong verbal and written communication skills, able to manage multiple stakeholders and creatively align their interests, and able to lead workshops on ideation/requirements with little preparation Strong organizational and time management skills; ability to multi-task and driving teams to meet aggressive delivery commitments Proactive problem-solving and information research skills Ability to manage/mentor earlier tenure and/or less experienced staff to help grow future leaders and deepen value stream knowledge on the broader team Ability to accept change in stride, remaining flexible due to changing business needs/priorities Natural ‘teacher’, able to coach business partners on optimal use of data assets to meet stated/latent business needs Comfort with/knowledge of analytic tools such as Tableau, PowerBI, Databricks, SQL, etc. Deep knowledge/experience with data lakehouse principles and data management tooling (e.g., Databricks, Fivetran, Collibra, SQL/SparkSQL/Python, etc.) Able to see the “big picture” and influence/engage others to meet common goals You Will: Engage in business value stream Counseling: Develop deep and trust-based relationships with value stream stakeholders Consult with value stream business partners to identify and shape new and existing ways for data to enable business processes and aspirations Lead decisioning/status, prioritization, and ideation meetings with various stakeholder groups, from executives to expert practitioners as it relates to new data/info products and/or data service delivery Maintain a data product and info product roadmap supported by technical capabilities to keep business abreast of vision and timing Capture demand and stay responsive to emergent needs from business value stream business partners (from the value stream leads to the application PDMs) Demonstrate expertise in processes and their supporting data assets by educating business partners (directly 1:1, in formal training sessions, and informally), and by providing direct feedback to value stream owners on usage characteristics of value stream data solutions Product Management: Serve as the primary owner of FPRS/CSWM value stream data product strategy, managing the roadmap for ongoing enhancements and new capabilities Accountable for the overall agile delivery of new data sources and products, establishing the features, business rules and test conditions with business input, and in response, coordinating the explosion of these features into technical detail via your team of DPOs In concert with EDSO platform engineering team members, contribute to the evaluation of proposed tools, technologies, and platforms from a business lens Contribute to ongoing delivery/capacity planning, ensuring value stream needs are appropriately resourced/planned for delivery with eDSO solution engineers/architects Manage testing, both directly as a point tester and via coordination with key business SMEs Partner with value stream leaders and PDMs to co-drive data solution adoption, ensuring the appropriate change management and/or training needs are met such that solution use drives desired business outcomes Governance and Compliance: Ensure solutions adhere to industry regulations, data security standards, and organizational policies Monitor and mitigate risks related to solution implementation Direct matrixed data stewards to craft collect, maintain and troubleshoot critical data element metadata and data quality Location: Location: flexible: New York City preferred; Bethlehem, PA; Holmdel, NJ are also options Job Title: Head of IT and Cyber Risk
Job Description: Reporting to the Head of Operational Risk, the Head of IT & Cyber Risk provides strategic leadership for designing, implementing, and continuously enhancing second line technology and cyber risk frameworks. You will ensure the organization proactively identifies, assesses, and manages technology and cybersecurity risks, working with stakeholders to embed resiliency and robust risk practices into business operations. You will lead a specialized risk team focused on risk assessment, technology resiliency initiatives, and ongoing improvement of risk methodologies. You Will Lead and oversee comprehensive IT and cyber risk assessments aligned to industry frameworks including ( e.g.   National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) and International Organization for Standardization, (ISO)) to identify, evaluate, and prioritize risk exposures across the enterprise, including ownership of the annual NYDFS Cybersecurity Risk Assessment process, including proper documentation and reporting. Drive execution and oversight of technology resiliency initiatives, including development and regular testing of business continuity and disaster recovery plans. Collaborate with 1st and 3rd lines of defense to oversee the completion of control testing, logging findings, and integrate results into risk assessments and dashboards. Partner with auditors to support SOC attestation and remediation. Champion integration of risk management processes and reporting within ServiceNow (SN), ensuring seamless connectivity, process automation, and unified oversight across platforms. Develop, maintain, and present dashboards and key metrics to senior stakeholders providing visibility into risk landscape, control effectiveness, and program performance, enabling data-driven decision making. Own and maintain the IT Risk Register, facilitate root cause analysis and lessons learned, and oversee remediation activities to continuously strengthen the control environment. Partner with the third-party risk and business risk & resiliency teams to comprehensive IT and cyber risk assessments are performed and remediation efforts are implemented. You Are Driven to accelerate impact and lead change Exceptional communicator across multiple levels of an organization, able to drive outcomes through others Flexible and resourceful in managing multiple priorities You Have 10+ years of experience in cybersecurity, IT, or information security, with at least 5 years in risk management or leadership role. Deep knowledge of risk management frameworks (NIST, ISO, COBIT), incident response, control design, and regulatory compliance is required Certifications preferred (ISM, CISSP, or similar) Reporting relationships This position reports to the Head of Operational Risk, who reports to our Chief Risk Officer within Enterprise Risk Management. Job Title: Platform Owner, Finance Technology (Oracle ERP/EPM, Kyriba)
Job Description: Position Summary T he Platform Owner , Finance Technology is responsible for the strategy , governance, lifecycle, and performance outcomes of Guardian’s financial technology ecosystem. This includes Oracle ERP, Oracle EPM , Kyriba, and a suite of supporting financial applications that enable accounting , FP&A, treasury, tax, procurement, and controllership functions. This leader ensures that Guardian’s financial systems are reliable, scalable, well ‑ governed, and continuously evolving to support both the company’s current operations and the Journey to ’30 growth and transformation agenda. The role blends strategic leadership with hands ‑ on oversight of system architecture, process optimization, and cross ‑ functional enablement and coordination . Serves as the decision owner for platform architecture, configuration standards, and major design tradeoffs across the Oracle financial ecosystem, optimizing enterprise outcomes over individual functional preferences . Key Responsibilities Strategic Leadership Support the Head of Finance Technology to d efine and execute the multi ‑ year roadmap for Guardian’s financial systems, aligned with Finance’s modernization strategy and enterprise technology standards. Own the platform architecture vision and guardrails (configuration patterns, integration standards, data controls) to ensure scalable, reusable solutions across Finance functions. Serve as the escalation point for complex platform decisions and systemic risks (e.g., cross‑module design tradeoffs, integration failures, environment/release constraints), driving resolution and long-term remediation. Serve as the primary technology advisor and decision owner for platform architecture, configuration standards, and integration guardrails to the Head of Finance Technology on system capabilities, process redesign opportunities, and emerging technologies. Establish governance frameworks for financial systems, ensuring compliance, data integrity, and strong internal controls. Systems Ownership & Delivery Oversee the end ‑ to ‑ end lifecycle of Oracle ERP, Oracle EPM , Kyriba, and related financial applications, including architecture, configuration, integrations, and upgrades. Establish and run platform governance routines (architecture/design reviews, release readiness, change controls) to ensure consistency, auditability, and quality of platform changes. Define and track platform health metrics (availability, incident trends, integration reliability, change failure rate, performance, and security posture) and regularly communicate insights and actions to stakeholders. Drive root-cause resolution for recurring issues by prioritizing systemic fixes (standardization, automation, simplification) over one-off workarounds. Partner with IT, Cybersecurity, Data, Internal Audit, and Enterprise Architecture teams to ensure system reliability, security, and alignment with enterprise standards. Drive continuous improvement initiatives, including automation, workflow enhancements, and analytics enablement. Team Leadership & Vendor Management Manage and develop a high ‑ performing team of financial systems professionals, including functional leads, analysts, and administrators. Build a culture of accountability, collaboration, and customer ‑ centricity within the team. Oversee relationships with key technology partners, including Oracle, Kyriba, and implementation/support vendors (Deloitte and others). Stakeholder Engagement Act as the primary liaison between Finance and Technology teams, ensuring clear communication, platform intake, governance-based prioritization, and coordination of delivery of platform capabilities. Partner closely with Controllership, FP&A, Treasury, Procurement, and Tax to understand needs and translate them into scalable system solutions. Support Finance leadership with insights on system performance, risks, and opportunities. Provide shared platform capabilities, standards, and guardrails to enable Finance technology teams and downstream products—while functional/domain teams retain accountability for business outcomes and product delivery . Platform Governance & Operational Excellence Establish and run platform governance routines (architecture/design reviews, release readiness, change controls) to ensure consistency, auditability, and quality of platform changes. Define and track platform health metrics (availability, incident trends, integration reliability, change failure rate, performance, and security posture) and regularly communicate insights and actions to stakeholders. Drive root-cause resolution for recurring issues by prioritizing systemic fixes (standardization, automation, simplification) over one-off workarounds. Qualifications 1 5 + years of experience in financial systems leadership roles, with deep expertise in Oracle ERP and Oracle EPM. Strong understanding of Kyriba or comparable treasury management systems. Proven experience leading mid ‑ sized teams and managing complex system landscapes. Demonstrated success driving process transformation and system modernization in a large or matrixed organization. Strong knowledge of financial processes, internal controls, and regulatory requirements. Excellent communication skills and the ability to influence senior stakeholders. Experience in insurance, financial services, or other highly regulated industries. Background in cloud ERP/EPM implementations or major system upgrades. Familiarity with integration platforms, data governance, and analytics tools. Project management or change management certification (PMP, Prosci , etc.). Job Title: Strategic Data Initiative Lead Enterprise Data Solutions & Operations (EDSO), Guardian Digital & Technology (D&T)
Job Description: Strategic Data Initiative Lead Enterprise Data Solutions & Operations (EDSO), Guardian Digital & Technology (D&T) Job Description The Strategic Data Initiative Lead  is a critical role responsible for orchestrating and executing the company’s most pivotal, large-scale transformation initiatives. This individual will operate at the nexus of strategy and execution, responsible for not only the meticulous, hands-on delivery of complex programs but also for crafting and communicating critical program level narratives related to status, budget and progress to C-suite executives and senior leaders. ​ Reporting to the Head of Strategic Data Initiatives this individual will guide cross-functional teams in a matrixed environment to deliver transformational changes that reshape our business processes, technology platforms, and competitive positioning. The ideal candidate is a seasoned leader with a proven history of managing ambiguous, high-impact data transformation programs from conception to completion within a large complex enterprise, preferably in an insurance context. You will ​​Lead the end-to-end execution of large-scale data transformation programs ensuring they are delivered on-scope, on-time, and on-budget Facilitate collaboration between disparate business, technology, and operational teams to ensure seamless integration and alignment. Establish and manage robust program governance, including risk and dependency management, issue resolution, resource planning, and detailed project milestone tracking. ​Directly manage program-level budgets, forecasting, and resource allocation to ensure efficient use of capital and headcount. ​​Develop and articulate a compelling strategic narratives for approaches to transformation initiatives, translating complex program details into clear, concise, and persuasive stories for an executive audience. ​Create high-impact presentation materials (decks) and communication artifacts to rally C-suite and senior leadership around the approach to delivery of a program's vision, as well as reporting on progress. ​Serve as a partner to critical stakeholders, proactively managing expectations and ensuring continuous alignment through rigorous communications and transparency in delivery processes. Form relationships with critical business and technology stakeholders and establish a trusted working relationship. ​You Have ​​Bachelor's degree in Business, Technology, or a related field. ​8+ years of progressive experience in program management, with a demonstrated track record of successfully leading enterprise-wide business or digital transformation initiatives. ​Proven experience leading large-scale programs in a complex and regulated industry (e.g., Financial Services, Banking, Healthcare). ​Exceptional executive-level communication, presentation, and storytelling skills, with the ability to distill complexity into clarity and influence senior decision-makers. ​Mastery of leading through influence in a highly matrixed organization, with a demonstrated ability to build consensus and drive results without direct authority. Preferred: ​Direct experience with Life Insurance, Disability Insurance, and Dental Insurance ​MBA or other advanced degree. Measures of Success: ​Performance in this role will be evaluated on a balanced set of outcomes, including: ​Business Value Realization: Achievement of defined business outcomes and strategic goals (e.g., market launch of new products, realization of expense efficiencies, successful platform modernization). ​Program Delivery Excellence: Consistent on-time and on-budget delivery of major program milestones and deliverables. ​Stakeholder Satisfaction & Organizational Adoption: Positive feedback from executive sponsors and key stakeholders, coupled with the successful adoption and embedding of new processes, technologies, and ways of working across the organization. Job Title: 2026 Early Career Program- Corporate Finance & Risk Rotational Program, Enterprise Finance
Job Description: 2026 Early Career Program- Corporate Finance & Risk Rotational Program , Enterprise Finance Are you looking for a career in finance a t an established company whose purpose is to inspire well-being ? If yes, come join the Corporate Finance organization through our Corporate Finance & Risk Rotational Program . This unique two-year rotational position provides you with the opportunity to work in a variety of accounting , risk, and financial divisions and provides you with the knowledge and skills to build a rewarding career. You can expect to learn about Guardian and get hands-on experience in how finance , audit and risk are an integral partner to all areas of our business. This position will rotate across the Corporate Finance & Risk organization, focusing on one department or project every year . At the conclusion of the program, we anticipate successful candidates to transition into a full-time role within the Corporate Finance & R isk team . Location: New York, NY You are: A senior with an anticipated graduation date of May 202 6 , preferably with a degree in Economics, Finance, Accounting, Business, Audit, Risk, or Mathematics A team player with a can-do attitude who is looking to make a positive impact You have: A high level of comfort with finance and accounting principles Analytical and creative problem-solving skills Strong proficiency with Microsoft Excel Demonstrated ability to deliver timely results Knowledge of Oracle General Ledger and SmartView a plus You will: Assist in preparations for various reports, work papers, provisions and reconciliations Assist in the preparation of quarterly financial statements and year-end accounting functions Assist in Compensation & Benefits related journal entries, reconciliations, and reporting Assist in various Position Management functions including updating position status, reconciliations between systems, reporting, and ongoing maintenance. Help team leads with the year-end and month-end planning process Assist with completing and respon ding to various audit requests Assist in tracking and reporting expenses, expense analysis, financial statement analysis, journal entries and various accounting functions Help to build out reports in Oracle Assist in process improvement projects to enhance risk, accounting and reporting efficiencies Have the opportunity to work and learn from supportive leaders, mentors and team members across the organization who will help coach you as you develop your professional career Learn about Guardian’s purpose, values, how we work, and our suite of product and service offerings Build a network of colleagues and have a sense of community with other parts of the business Think broadly and ask questions about data, facts and other information Be a self-starter – someone who enjoys “rolling up their sleeves and getting things done ”, has high energy, strong work ethic, displays the ability to work independently, and is creative We o ffe r: Meaningful and challenging work opportunities to accelerate innovation in a secure and compliant way Dynamic, modern work environments that promote collaboration and creativity to develop and empower talent Employee Resource Groups that advocate for inclusion and diversity in all that we do Social responsibility in all aspects of our work- we volunteer within our local communities, create educational alliances with colleges, and drive a variety of initiatives in sustainability Eligibility: Applicants must be eligible to work in the U.S. without company sponsorship, now or in the future, for employment-based work authorization. F-1 visa holders with Optional Practical Training (OPT) who will require H-1B status, TNs, or current H-1B visa holders will not be considered.  H1-B and green card sponsorship are not available for this position.
